outbound_official.notes: Qatar's Ministry of Foreign Affairs publishes per-country visa requirements for Qatar CITIZENS traveling abroad via the 'Qatar and the World' section (mofa.gov.qa/en/qatar-and-the-world?country=XX). Each country page states whether a Qatari regular passport holder 'is not required to apply for visa' (visa-free), can obtain a visa 'at airport/borders upon arrival' (visa on arrival), or 'must obtain from nearest embassy before departure' (prior visa). There is no single consolidated list page - data is per-country only. Lists below reflect countries explicitly checked from official MOFA pages.

name: Qatar

visa_types.name: Hayya Tourist E-Visa (A1)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism and leisure visits to Qatar applied for online via the Hayya platform before travel.
visa_types.notes: Fee is QAR 100 (approx. USD 27). Extendable by an additional 30 days from inside Qatar. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from arrival. Return/onward ticket required. Mandatory health insurance from MOH-registered providers required. Available via Hayya portal or mobile app.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival
visa_types.purpose: Entry permit issued at Qatar's ports of entry for eligible nationalities for tourism, transit, or short visits without prior application.
visa_types.notes: Paid visa on arrival (QAR 100 / approx. USD 27) available to nationals of Belarus, Iran, Macau, Mauritius, Montenegro, and Taiwan. Stay 30 days extendable by another 30 days. Iran requires hotel reservation or invitation letter. Belarus requires credit/debit card with USD 1,000+ balance. Separately, over 100 nationalities receive a FREE on-arrival entry permit (no QAR 100 fee) - see visa_policy.visa_free entries.

visa_types.name: Joint Tourist Visa - Qatar & Oman
visa_types.purpose: Combined tourist visa allowing holders to visit both Qatar and Oman on a single visa, issued through consular channels.
visa_types.notes: Fee QAR 100 (approx. USD 27) per MOFA fee schedule. Applied for through Qatari embassies/consulates or Omani equivalents. Exact stay duration and validity not published on official source consulted.

visa_types.name: Businessmen Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreign nationals travelling to Qatar for business meetings, conferences, or commercial activities.
visa_types.notes: Fee QAR 200 (approx. USD 55) per MOFA official fee schedule - the highest published fee among visitor visa categories. Applied for through Qatari embassies/consulates or via the Hayya e-platform. Exact stay duration and validity not published on official source consulted.

visa_types.name: Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: General-purpose short-stay visitor visa for foreign nationals visiting Qatar for personal, family, or social reasons.
visa_types.notes: Fee QAR 100 (approx. USD 27) per MOFA official fee schedule. Can be applied for through Qatari embassies/consulates or the MOI e-services portal. Extendable inside Qatar. The Consular Affairs Department handles cases not eligible for electronic visa system.

visa_types.name: GCC Residents Visit Visa (Hayya A2)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign nationals holding a valid GCC country (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, or UAE) residence permit who wish to visit Qatar.
visa_types.notes: Fee QAR 100 (approx. USD 27). GCC resident permit must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Passport must also be valid for at least 3 months from arrival. Applicant must be travelling from their country of GCC residency. Job title on application must match permit. Extendable. Also available on arrival at ports of entry. Hayya A2 category.

visa_types.name: Companion of GCC National Visa (Hayya A4)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for individuals accompanying GCC citizens travelling to Qatar.
visa_types.notes: Fee QAR 100 (approx. USD 27) per MOFA fee schedule. Issued to companions (family members or associates) of GCC national travellers. Also described as 'Entry visa for companions of GCC Nationals' in MOFA fee table. Hayya A4 category.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay transit visa for passengers transiting through Qatar's Hamad International Airport without leaving the transit zone or during a stopover.
visa_types.notes: Fee QAR 100 (approx. USD 27) per MOFA official fee schedule. Applied for through Qatari embassies/consulates. Qatar Airways passengers may be eligible for complimentary Doha stopover packages. Exact stay duration not published on MOFA page consulted - 4 days is commonly referenced for Qatar transit but not confirmed on official source fetched.

visa_types.name: Electronic Travel Authorization / ETA-Backed E-Visa (Hayya A3)
visa_types.purpose: Online e-visa for nationals of countries not otherwise eligible for visa-free entry, if they hold a valid visa or residency permit from the US, UK, Canada, Schengen area, Australia, or New Zealand.
visa_types.notes: Fee QAR 100 (approx. USD 27). Credential (third-country visa or residency) must be valid at time of Hayya e-visa issuance, not merely at travel date. Qualifying credentials: valid US, UK, Canadian, Schengen, Australian, or New Zealand visa or permanent residency. Passport valid 3+ months from arrival required. Return ticket required. Hayya A3 category.

visa_types.name: Official / Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visa issued to foreign officials, diplomats, and government representatives travelling to Qatar on official business.
visa_types.notes: Fee exempted per MOFA official fee schedule. Issued through diplomatic channels to holders of diplomatic, official, or special passports. Many nationalities have bilateral agreements with Qatar exempting diplomatic/official/special passport holders entirely (e.g., Thailand per March 2025 agreement).

visa_types.name: Work Visa / Employment Entry Permit
visa_types.purpose: Entry permit for foreign nationals sponsored by a Qatari employer, allowing the holder to enter Qatar to take up employment.
visa_types.notes: Qatar operates a kafala-derived sponsorship system. A Qatari employer (sponsor) must apply on the worker's behalf through the MOI e-services portal. Fee and processing time not published on accessible official pages. Upon arrival, worker obtains a Qatar ID (QID). Work visa tracking available at portal.moi.gov.qa visa inquiry service. Fees and processing times not confirmed from official pages fetched.

visa_types.name: Family Visa / Family Reunification (Host Family & Friends)
visa_types.purpose: Visa enabling Qatar residents to sponsor visits by family members or friends to Qatar for short stays.
visa_types.notes: Hayya platform offers a 'Host Family & Friends' feature allowing Qatar residents to sponsor short-stay visit visas for their family and friends. Fee QAR 100 (approx. USD 27). Duration and validity follow standard visit visa conditions (30 days, extendable). Full details require login to Hayya portal.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Paid visa on arrival, QAR 100 fee, 30 days extendable by another 30 days. Credit/debit card with USD 1,000+ balance required as financial proof. Previously unconfirmed from official page - now confirmed via Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) citing separate paid-VOA tier.

Paid visa on arrival, QAR 100 fee, 30 days extendable by another 30 days. Hotel reservation or invitation letter required. Confirmed in paid VOA tier by Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) - separate from the free-entry group.

Macau SAR passport holders. Paid visa on arrival, QAR 100 fee, 30 days extendable by another 30 days. Confirmed via Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) in the paid-VOA tier. Previously unconfirmed from official page.
Paid visa on arrival, QAR 100 fee, 30 days extendable by another 30 days. Confirmed via Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) in the paid-VOA tier. Previously unconfirmed from official page.

Taiwan (Republic of China) passport holders. Paid visa on arrival, QAR 100 fee, 30 days extendable by another 30 days. Confirmed via Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) in the paid-VOA tier. Previously unconfirmed from official page.

suspended.notes: Visa-on-arrival (free tier) temporarily suspended as of 31 March 2026 per advisory from Pakistani Embassy in Doha, citing prevailing regional situation. Pakistani nationals must now obtain a pre-approved visa via the Hayya platform before travel. No resumption date announced as of June 2026. Wikipedia classifies Pakistan in the 30-day visa-free tier but notes it as suspended.

Visa-on-arrival (free tier) suspended effective 2 April 2026. Lebanese citizens must obtain a pre-approved visa through the Hayya platform before departure. Suspension indefinite - no resumption date as of June 2026. Confirmed via EY Global Tax News citing official Qatar government advisory. Wikipedia classifies Lebanon in the 30-day visa-free tier but notes it as suspended.

visa_policy.notes: Qatar's entry permit system is administered through the Ministry of Interior (MOI) and the Hayya e-platform (hayya.qa). Over 100 nationalities receive a FREE on-arrival entry permit (no fee). GCC citizens enter visa-free with no stay limit. Most other eligible nationalities receive a free on-arrival tourist entry permit. 90-day stay is granted to eligible EU member states and selected other nationalities; 30-day stay (extendable) applies to most other eligible nationalities. A separate paid visa-on-arrival tier (QAR 100) applies to Belarus, Iran, Macau, Mauritius, Montenegro, and Taiwan. India, Thailand, and Ukraine must pre-book hotel accommodation through the Discover Qatar platform for on-arrival entry eligibility. Pakistan and Lebanon free on-arrival entry temporarily suspended as of March-April 2026. The Hayya platform offers tourist e-visas (A1), GCC resident visa (A2), ETA-backed visa for holders of major country visas/residency (A3), and companion of GCC citizen visa (A4). All rules are subject to change without notice per portal.moi.gov.qa.

credential.subtype: any valid GCC residence permit (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, or UAE) with approved profession

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must have a valid GCC residence permit valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Applicant must be travelling to Qatar from their country of GCC residency. Return flight ticket required. Fee QAR 100. Single entry. Extendable. Job title on application must match title on residence permit (Hayya dropdown list). On-arrival visa also available at port of entry. Hayya A2 e-visa applies.

credential.subtype: any valid US visa or US residency permit/Green Card

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must have a valid US visa or US residency at the time of Hayya e-visa issuance (not merely at time of travel). Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Return flight ticket required. Fee QAR 100. Single entry. Hayya A3 (Visa with ETA) applies. Credential must be valid when e-visa is received, not only at travel date. This applies to nationalities that do NOT otherwise qualify for unconditional visa-free or visa-on-arrival entry.

credential.subtype: any valid UK visa or UK residency

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must have a valid UK visa or UK residency at the time of Hayya e-visa issuance. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Return flight ticket required. Fee QAR 100. Single entry. Hayya A3 (Visa with ETA) applies. Credential must be valid when e-visa is received, not only at travel date.

credential.subtype: any valid Canadian visa or Canadian residency

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must have a valid Canadian visa or Canadian residency at the time of Hayya e-visa issuance. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Return flight ticket required. Fee QAR 100. Single entry. Hayya A3 (Visa with ETA) applies. Credential must be valid when e-visa is received, not only at travel date.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa or Schengen area residency permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must have a valid Schengen visa or Schengen area residency at the time of Hayya e-visa issuance. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Return flight ticket required. Fee QAR 100. Single entry. Hayya A3 (Visa with ETA) applies. Credential must be valid when e-visa is received, not only at travel date.

credential.subtype: any valid Australian visa or Australian residency

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must have a valid Australian visa or Australian residency at the time of Hayya e-visa issuance. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Return flight ticket required. Fee QAR 100. Single entry. Hayya A3 (Visa with ETA) applies. Credential must be valid when e-visa is received, not only at travel date.

credential.subtype: any valid New Zealand visa or New Zealand residency

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must have a valid New Zealand visa or New Zealand residency at the time of Hayya e-visa issuance. Passport must be valid for at least 3 months from date of arrival. Return flight ticket required. Fee QAR 100. Single entry. Hayya A3 (Visa with ETA) applies. Credential must be valid when e-visa is received, not only at travel date.

Bilateral agreement signed 3 March 2025 between State of Qatar and Kingdom of Thailand exempting holders of diplomatic, official, and special passports from visa requirements. Stay duration not published in official press release. Agreement signed by Qatar's Minister of State for Foreign Affairs Sultan bin Saad Al Muraikhi and Thailand's Foreign Minister Maris Sangiampongsa. Note: Thai ORDINARY passport holders are separately eligible for free on-arrival entry (30 days) with mandatory Discover Qatar hotel pre-booking - not affected by this diplomatic agreement.

Nationals of India, Iran, Thailand, and Ukraine qualify for free on-arrival entry (30 days extendable) but must pre-book hotel accommodation through the Discover Qatar platform for their entire stay prior to travel. Financial proof required for India and Iran (credit/debit card with ~QAR 5,000 / USD 1,000 equivalent, or 3-month bank statement). Iran also requires hotel reservation or invitation letter. Iran is separately subject to the paid-VOA QAR 100 tier per Wikipedia classification - classification uncertainty noted in gaps. Pakistan visa-on-arrival suspended March 2026 and is not in this list. Ukraine is in the 90-day free tier with hotel pre-booking required per aggregator sources.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on official Qatar government sources (moi.gov.qa, mofa.gov.qa).

agreements: GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) - citizens of Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, UAE enter Qatar visa-free with no published maximum stay
Qatar-China bilateral visa exemption agreement - entered into force December 2018
Qatar-Japan mutual visa exemption - exchange of official memorandums signed 2018/2019
Qatar-Thailand bilateral agreement (3 March 2025) - diplomatic, official, special passport holders exempt from visa requirements

validation_notes: CORRECTED: visa_policy.visa_free and visa_on_arrival were empty arrays in prior record - populated with 5 GCC visa_free entries and ~80 visa_on_arrival entries for nationalities across two stay-duration tiers.
CORRECTED: Prior record noted '95+ countries' in notes but had no structured entries - now structured with individual nationality entries for all confirmed countries.

CORRECTED: Stay duration split added - 90-day group (US, EU members, Argentina, Brazil, Russia, South Africa, South Korea, Turkey, Ukraine, etc.) vs 30-day group (UK, Canada, Australia, China, Japan, Singapore, etc.) - prior record treated all as 30 days or left unspecified.
CORRECTED: US citizens now correctly documented as receiving a 2-year multiple-entry visa with 90-day stays per visit (not 30 days as implied by old Hayya F1 categorization).
ADDED: Pakistan visa-on-arrival suspension effective 31 March 2026.
ADDED: Lebanon visa-on-arrival suspension effective 2 April 2026.
ADDED: India, Iran, Thailand Discover Qatar hotel pre-booking requirement noted in conditional_access as special_status.
ADDED: Suspended array in visa_policy to track nationalities with temporary suspensions.
RETAINED: Hayya A2/A3 conditional_access credential entries (GCC resident visa, US/UK/Canada/Schengen/Australia/NZ visa-holders) unchanged as these were confirmed accurate.
RETAINED: Thailand diplomatic/official/special passport visa-free agreement (March 2025) - confirmed via MOFA official source.

NOTE: visitqatar.com (Qatar Tourism, government tourism board) used as supplementary source alongside portal.moi.gov.qa and hayya.qa for the nationality lists, as MOI portal pages return dynamic/gated content.

VERIFIED 2026-06-14: India (IND) correctly classified as visa_on_arrival with mandatory Discover Qatar hotel pre-booking condition - NOT visa_free. Aggregator claim of 'visa_free or visa_on_arrival' is misleading; hotel pre-booking via discoverqatar.qa is required by MOI. Source: portal.moi.gov.qa (MOI) and discoverqatar.qa (Discover Qatar mandatory hotels page). India remains in conditional_access[special_status] with level=visa_on_arrival and is absent from visa_policy.visa_free - no changes to policy entries needed, only this verification note added.

ADDED 2026-06-20: visa_types array populated with 12 entries covering all officially documented Qatar visa categories. Sources: mofa.gov.qa/en/consular-services/visas (fee schedule), hayya.qa (Hayya platform A1/A2/A3/A4), portal.moi.gov.qa (MOI entry permits). Categories covered: tourist (A1/visa-on-arrival/joint Qatar-Oman), business (businessmen visa), visit visa, GCC residents visa (A2), companion of GCC national (A4), transit, ETA-backed (A3), diplomatic/official, work visa (employer-sponsored), family/host visa.

CORRECTED 2026-06-27: Iran (IRN) added to visa_policy.visa_on_arrival - it was previously missing from the structured visa_on_arrival list and only appeared in conditional_access[special_status]. Iran is eligible for visa on arrival (30 days, QAR 100 fee) with mandatory Discover Qatar hotel pre-booking and financial proof. Confirmed via multiple aggregators citing visitqatar.com/MOI - same tier as India and Thailand.

CORRECTED 2026-06-27: conditional_access[special_status] updated to include India (previously only listed Iran and Thailand), and conditions text updated to reflect confirmed financial proof requirements (QAR 5,000 / 1,000 USD).

CORRECTED 2026-06-27: Thailand ordinary passport visa-on-arrival confirmed ACTIVE as of June 2026 - prior record contained an erroneous note in conditional_access passport_type entry stating ordinary VOA 'is currently suspended'. This was incorrect; Thai ordinary passport holders can obtain VOA with hotel pre-booking. Note corrected.

CORRECTED 2026-06-27: India notes in visa_on_arrival cleaned up - removed stale text about Pakistan suspension and Thailand diplomatic agreement uncertainty that was incorrectly embedded in India's notes field.

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Pakistan and Lebanon visa-on-arrival suspensions remain in effect. No resumption dates announced. Confirmed via travelandtourworld.com, wego.com, lexismiddleeast.com, businesstoday.in.
VERIFIED 2026-06-27: GCC citizens (BHR, KWT, OMN, SAU, ARE) visa-free entry confirmed - no changes needed.

STRUCTURAL CORRECTION 2026-06-29: The large group previously classified as visa_on_arrival (all nationalities receiving FREE on-arrival entry permits) has been reclassified to visa_free. This reflects Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) and visitqatar.com (which now explicitly states 'visa-free entry for 102 nationalities') distinguishing between: (1) FREE on-arrival entry permits = visa_free in our schema (no QAR 100 fee), and (2) PAID on-arrival visas (QAR 100) = visa_on_arrival in our schema. Sources: Wikipedia Visa policy of Qatar, visitqatar.com/intl-en/plan-your-trip/visas.

CORRECTED 2026-06-29: Belarus (BLR), Macau (MAC iso3=null), Mauritius (MUS), Montenegro (MNE), Taiwan (TWN) added to visa_policy.visa_on_arrival (paid QAR 100 tier). These were previously in data_quality.gaps as unconfirmed. Now confirmed via Wikipedia's dedicated paid-VOA tier for these nationalities.

CORRECTED 2026-06-29: Iran (IRN) moved from visa_policy.visa_on_arrival (where it was added 2026-06-27 as implied paid tier) - however classification tension exists: Wikipedia places Iran in paid-VOA tier (QAR 100) while previous records placed it in a special-conditions group. Iran kept in visa_on_arrival (paid, QAR 100) per Wikipedia which is most recent synthesis.

CORRECTED 2026-06-29: India (IND) reclassified from visa_policy.visa_on_arrival to visa_policy.visa_free - Wikipedia now explicitly lists India in the 30-day visa-free (no fee) tier, with hotel pre-booking condition noted. This updates the June 2026-06-14 note that kept India out of visa_free - the policy appears to have changed terminology or India's fee status was clarified.

CORRECTED 2026-06-29: Ukraine (UKR) hotel pre-booking condition via Discover Qatar added - aggregator source (wego) now includes Ukraine alongside India and Thailand in the hotel pre-booking requirement group.
VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Pakistan and Lebanon suspensions remain confirmed with no restoration announced. Source: EY Global Tax News (official-level reporting), wego.com.
gaps: Comprehensive official list of all visa-free eligible countries not directly accessible from portal.moi.gov.qa dynamic pages - nationality list cross-verified from Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) and visitqatar.com (government tourism board).
Exact stay duration for GCC citizens not published - no maximum stay limit identified for GCC nationals (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, UAE).
Maximum stay for Thailand diplomatic/official/special passport holders: the official MOFA press release does not state the maximum permitted stay duration.

Complete list of bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport visa waiver agreements: Qatar likely has bilateral agreements with many more countries beyond Thailand (e.g., Arab League members, GCC, other bilateral partners) but no comprehensive official list is publicly enumerated on mofa.gov.qa public pages.

Discover Qatar platform exact requirements for India, Iran, Thailand, Ukraine visa-free entry: specific hotel booking process and whether it also applies to additional nationalities were not confirmed from MOI official page. Financial proof threshold (QAR 5,000 or 1,000 USD) confirmed via aggregator sources citing MOI, not from a direct official page fetch.

Pakistan and Lebanon suspension end dates: both suspensions are indefinite with no official government press release found on portal.moi.gov.qa or mofa.gov.qa directly - suspension confirmed via EY Global Tax News and travel news aggregators.

Iran classification tension: Wikipedia places Iran in the paid-VOA tier (QAR 100) while it also appears in the special-conditions hotel pre-booking group. The two conditions (paid QAR 100 + hotel pre-booking) may both apply simultaneously. Exact official requirements for Iran not directly confirmed from portal.moi.gov.qa.
Belarus credit/debit card requirement: USD 1,000+ balance noted via Wikipedia - not confirmed from direct official Qatar government source.
Macau, Mauritius, Montenegro, Taiwan in paid-VOA tier: confirmed via Wikipedia only - not verified from portal.moi.gov.qa or hayya.qa direct fetch during this session.
A3 credential subtype: Hayya FAQ states 'valid visa or residency' without specifying visa subclasses (e.g., B1/B2, F-1, Tier 2). All subtypes appear to qualify; exact subtype list not published.
GCC Resident Visa (A2) eligible professions: Hayya FAQ states job title on permit must be on the dropdown list; full eligible professions list not published on official source found.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder entry rules: No official Qatar government source mentions OCI cardholder entry privileges - not recorded.
Work visa fees and processing times: MOI portal pages return 'content expired' for work visa section - fee schedule and timelines not confirmed from official source.

Student visa: No dedicated student visa category found on official Qatar government sources consulted. Students may enter on visit/work visa; specific student visa details not published on accessible pages.
Medical visa: No dedicated medical visa category listed in official MOFA or MOI fee schedules consulted.
Transit visa exact duration: MOFA page lists transit visa at QAR 100 but does not specify maximum transit stay; 4-day figure is commonly referenced but not confirmed from official source fetched.
Businessmen visa exact stay duration and validity: MOFA page lists fee (QAR 200) but does not specify duration or validity on the consulted page.

Source authority for visa_free bulk entries: Sources were updated from visitqatar.com to Wikipedia (Visa policy of Qatar) for the reclassification - Wikipedia is not an official government source. The underlying data from visitqatar.com and portal.moi.gov.qa supports the free-entry classification but was not directly confirmed in a single parseable page during this session due to dynamic content limitations.
